response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack web and mobile engineer with 15+ years of experience, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role across every critical dimension. The position requires native-level Japanese proficiency (a non-negotiable hard filter), deep C programming for embedded UNIX firmware, Linux kernel-level investigation and modification, and hands-on embedded networking security experience (MACsec, 802.1X, Layer 2/3 switching). The candidate's core stack is TypeScript and Node.js for web and cloud platforms; his only C/C++ exposure is brief and dated (National Instruments, 2014–2015 in a desktop IDE context), and there is zero evidence of Japanese language ability, embedded security work, or switch/networking firmware experience.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience spanning web, mobile, and some embedded contexts, demonstrating adaptability
Demonstrated high-performance systems work at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second using async I/O and low-latency techniques in DLang
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and cross-team collaboration across hardware and software contexts
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency — a stated non-negotiable requirement that alone disqualifies the candidate
No embedded networking or security domain experience: no evidence of MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, Layer 2/3 networking, switch chips, or related security modules
C/C++ experience is minimal, dated (2014–2015), and limited to a desktop IDE product — no Linux kernel modification, embedded firmware, or chip-vendor SDK work

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security domain experience
